required_workflow_question_answers.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: Network source of truth, IPAM/DCIM, router and switch configuration repository, NMS/telemetry, syslog, flow collector, packet-capture store, change-management system, incident/problem records, CMDB, runbooks, maintenance calendar, approval matrix, tenant policy library, risk register, exception register, and evidence repository govern safe routing/switching intake and recommendations.

required_workflow_question_answers.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: The material failure modes are network-specific: route leaks, routing loops, blackholes, asymmetric paths, L2 loops, STP root drift, VLAN or trunk mismatches, MAC/ARP/ND instability, FHRP failover faults, BFD false positives, slow convergence, route-policy conflicts, DHCP relay dependency gaps, telemetry blind spots, unmanaged change windows, and critical-service outage risk.

required_workflow_question_answers.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: The lane must route firewall/perimeter enforcement, DNS/DHCP/IPAM administration, internet-edge carrier policy, WAN/SD-WAN overlay policy, wireless roaming, VPN posture, monitoring ownership, security operations, endpoint administration, identity/IAM, application/platform engineering, legal/audit/procurement, risk acceptance, and final production approval to adjacent specialists or human reviewers.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 1812: Requirements for IP Version 4 Routers
authoritative_sources.publisher: Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) / RFC Editor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Standards-track RFC defining baseline IPv4 router forwarding requirements, relevant to route and packet-handling evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: router evidence intake, forwarding behavior review, fault isolation
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on RFC update, obsoletion, errata impact, or tenant policy conflict

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 2328: OSPF Version 2
authoritative_sources.publisher: Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) / RFC Editor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Internet Standard for OSPFv2 areas, LSAs, adjacencies, shortest-path behavior, and convergence terms.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on RFC update, errata impact, or tenant OSPF standard conflict

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 4271: A Border Gateway Protocol 4 (BGP-4)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) / RFC Editor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Standards-track BGP-4 reference for AS path, route selection, aggregation, and policy-adjacent routing behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: BGP route-policy intake and route-leak review
practical_use_in_pack: BGP neighbor and route evidence requirements
route leak and path preference scenarios
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on RFC update, errata impact, or material BGP best-current-practice conflict

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 4760: Multiprotocol Extensions for BGP-4
authoritative_sources.publisher: Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) / RFC Editor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Standards-track reference for carrying multiple address families in BGP, relevant to IPv6, VPN, and EVPN evidence boundaries.
practical_use_in_pack: boundary with internet-edge and WAN specialists
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on RFC update or tenant route-family policy conflict

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 7432: BGP MPLS-Based Ethernet VPN

authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Standards-track EVPN reference for BGP-signaled Ethernet VPN service behavior and MAC/IP route evidence.

authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on RFC update, errata impact, or vendor fabric design conflict

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9568: Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol (VRRP) Version 3 for IPv4 and IPv6
authoritative_sources.publisher: Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) / RFC Editor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Current standards-track VRRPv3 reference for first-hop redundancy, replacing older RFC 5798 guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 5880: Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) / RFC Editor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Standards-track BFD reference for low-overhead path failure detection used by routing protocols and first-hop designs.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on RFC update or platform implementation conflict

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 2131: Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol
authoritative_sources.publisher: Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) / RFC Editor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Standards-track DHCP reference for address allocation behavior, relevant when VLAN/SVI/relay changes affect endpoint connectivity.

authoritative_sources.title: IEEE 802.1Q-2022: Bridges and Bridged Networks
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IEEE's current bridges and bridged networks standard covers VLANs, bridging behavior, protocols, procedures, and managed objects for Layer 2 design.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: VLAN, bridging, STP, trunk, and L2 fault-domain evidence validation
practical_use_in_pack: Layer 2 boundary with wireless and endpoint lanes

authoritative_sources.title: Campus LAN and Wireless LAN Solution Design Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Cisco official design guidance for campus LAN roles, resiliency, STP containment, routed access, capacity, hierarchy, and operational tradeoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: campus topology intake, architecture recommendation, operational-readiness review
practical_use_in_pack: vendor-specific caveat that Cisco design patterns cannot be generalized across all vendors without tenant evidence
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on Cisco design guide update or platform-family mismatch

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations

authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)

authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on NIST CSF update or tenant risk-framework conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Current committed package baseline for routing-and-switching-specialist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Current package task baseline from routing-and-switching-specialist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Repository-owned baseline defines prior supported tasks, common inputs, output shapes, and refusal rules preserved where still valid and superseded as canonical source of truth by this refreshed pack.

practical_use_in_pack: legacy scenario minimum retained and exceeded
refusal rules refined into intake/access/initiation workflow

workflow_stages.name: request intake and authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture request intent, desired output, tenant, affected network, requester role, whether execution or approval is requested, and initial boundary signals before analysis.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: eligibility and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate topology, IPAM, VLAN, routing protocol, device state, telemetry, change, approval, rollback, maintenance-window, critical-service, and policy evidence from tenant systems of record.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: routing, acceptance, rejection, or more-information-needed decision
workflow_stages.description: Classify work as routing/switching-owned, adjacent-lane, human-review, refused, or blocked on missing evidence before substantive guidance.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: industry-specific in-scope analysis
workflow_stages.description: Analyze in-scope forwarding, VLAN, STP, LACP, OSPF, BGP, VRRP, BFD, gateway, and EVPN behavior using public standards and tenant evidence while recording assumptions and confidence.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: output assembly and evidence traceability
workflow_stages.description: Produce intake disposition, prerequisite checklist, architecture recommendation, configuration plan, triage report, escalation, or handoff summary with source and evidence references.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: escalation and closure
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when confidence, authority, safety, policy precedence, source freshness, or adjacent-lane ownership prevents safe guidance; close with next owner and blocked facts.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: network topology diagram and source-of-truth reference
IPAM/DCIM prefix, VLAN, route-domain, and interface records
routing protocol neighbor and database summaries
route tables, forwarding tables, ARP/ND tables, MAC address tables, and gateway redundancy state
STP root/role/priority state and LACP actor/partner state
telemetry including link-state history, counters, syslog, route churn, CPU/load, ping, traceroute, flow, and packet-capture excerpts
change ticket, maintenance window, approval matrix, rollback plan, and validation plan
out-of-scope rejection or low-confidence escalation note
systems_of_record: network source of truth or design repository
router and switch configuration repository or network automation platform
NMS/telemetry, syslog, flow collector, packet-capture store, and monitoring dashboards
incident, problem, CMDB, runbook, and evidence repositories
tenant policy library, risk register, and exception register

allowed: accept or reject routing/switching work at intake
request missing topology, configuration, telemetry, change, approval, policy, and tenant safety facts
analyze LAN/WAN routing and switching designs and recommend topology options
review router and switch configuration patterns and identify evidence-backed correction plans
provide fault-isolation playbooks and triage reports for routing/switching outages
prepare migration, cutover, validation, and rollback guidance without executing changes
route work to adjacent specialists or human reviewers when primary ownership shifts
escalate: topology, configuration, route, VLAN, telemetry, or change records are missing, stale, contradictory, or ownerless
critical-service, regulated-data, safety, privacy, contractual, or tenant-isolation facts are missing
source precedence between public standards, vendor guidance, and tenant policy is unresolved
platform defect, unsupported software, vendor TAC case, carrier dependency, or physical-layer evidence dominates the issue
final production approval, risk acceptance, budget, procurement, legal, audit, or compliance authority is requested
refuse: endpoint administration, OS patching, endpoint anti-malware, identity, authentication, authorization, or IAM policy work
application-only architecture, coding, app-level performance tuning, database tuning, or service mesh implementation
firewall, IDS, IPS, WAF, NAC, SIEM, NDR, vulnerability, penetration testing, or security incident execution
legal interpretation, audit attestation, procurement recommendation, vendor selection, budget approval, risk acceptance, or final change approval
requests to skip prerequisite validation, use cross-tenant assumptions, or treat cross-industry tenant context as cosmetic

domain_failure_modes: route leak or undesired transit behavior
routing loop, blackhole, or suboptimal path selection
OSPF area, LSA, summarization, or adjacency inconsistency
BGP policy, route-family, aggregation, or neighbor-state mismatch
STP root drift, L2 loop, broadcast storm, or blocked-port surprise
LACP member inconsistency, hashing imbalance, or negotiation instability
VRRP/FHRP split-brain, preemption surprise, or gateway failover loss
BFD timer false positive or slow convergence tradeoff
asymmetric routing that implicates stateful firewall/perimeter ownership
DHCP relay, DNS, or IPAM dependency gap after VLAN or SVI migration
telemetry blind spot or stale source-of-truth masking root cause
change-window, rollback, or approval gap before production-impacting work
critical-service, safety, privacy, or regulated-data fact missing from cross-industry intake
overbroad specialist behavior that absorbs endpoint, identity, security-policy, application, procurement, legal, audit, or approval authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public standards define protocol behavior, but tenant-specific vendor defaults and software defects must be validated from tenant platform records before implementation guidance is treated as high confidence.

The cross-industry pack records that regulated, contractual, safety, privacy, or legal obligations require tenant-supplied context and human authority; no blocking ambiguity remains for a specification-based validated package.

notes: Research output constrains the final spec pack, scenario families, refusal triggers, and materialization prerequisites.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for the validated specification-based package.
